title: task-contract.v1
description: A bounded work order for Laguna XS.2 acting as a narrow worker, written by the laguna-task-contract skill to .laguna/task-contract.json at the workspace root. The schema enforces shape and completeness; the validator (scripts/validate_contract.ts) additionally enforces the bounded-contract rules: single-concern goal, bounded scope paths, task_type-consistent max_files_to_modify, safe local acceptance commands, and no unbounded verbs in the goal.

schema_version.description: Contract identifier; always the literal string "task-contract.v1".

model_mode.description: Always "laguna_xs_worker": this contract is consumed by Laguna XS.2 as a bounded worker. Router work belongs in router-contract.v1.

task_type.description: The one kind of work the worker performs. Read-only types (log_reduction, stack_trace_routing, repo_map) require scope.max_files_to_modify = 0; single_file_patch requires exactly 1; test_generation allows 1-2.

goal.description: Exactly one sentence naming one concern. No chained tasks (no '; ', ', and ', 'and then'), no unbounded quantifiers ('whole repo', 'everything', 'all tests').

context_packet.description: The curated context the worker receives instead of the full workspace. Empty arrays are explicit statements that a category is not needed.

files.description: Workspace-relative files the worker should read, each with a one-line reason.

why.description: Why this file is in the packet.

commands.description: Relevant commands (e.g. the failing command), verbatim.

logs.description: Workspace-relative paths of relevant logs or failure excerpts.

scope.description: The blast radius. paths lists what the worker may touch (read-only tasks: what it reads); max_files_to_modify caps edits.

paths.description: Explicit workspace-relative files or bounded globs. A glob is bounded only below a literal first segment (src/**/*.ts is fine; *, **, **/* and *.ts at the top level are not). No absolute paths, no '..'.

max_files_to_modify.description: Hard cap on files the worker may modify. 0 for read-only tasks, 1 for single_file_patch, 1-2 for test_generation.

output_format.description: The single allowed output shape. Patch tasks return only a unified diff; read-only tasks return JSON written to artifact_path.

artifact_path.description: Workspace-relative path where the JSON artifact lands. Required in practice when output_format is "json".

must_not.description: Explicit prohibitions, e.g. "change unrelated behavior", "perform broad refactors", "invent unavailable files".

acceptance.description: Mechanical checks that decide success. Every check must be runnable locally with no network access.

type.description: command/test_result need a concrete local command; schema needs schema_path; patch_apply needs target_path. The validator enforces these pairings.

command.description: Concrete shell command, verbatim and runnable — no placeholders ('...', '<file>', TODO), no network or destructive operations.

schema_path.description: Workspace-relative path of the JSON Schema the artifact must satisfy.

target_path.description: Workspace-relative file the patch must apply to.

max_repairs.description: Bounded repair loop: at most one repair attempt.

return_only_corrected_output.description: On repair, the worker returns only the corrected artifact — no explanations, no extra changes.

escalation.description: The explicit 'cannot complete this safely' path: what the worker does when acceptance still fails after the repair budget.
